topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the locker room hummed with an oppressive monotony. Liam meticulously wiped down his cleats for the tenth time, his movements jerky and too precise. Every glance toward the door felt like a physical pressure against his chest. He rehearsed the opening line in his head—confident, casual—but the words caught in his throat, turning to dust. He could feel the heat rising in his neck, a prickling sensation that made the fine hairs on his arms stand up. When the coach finally called the names, the air seemed to thin out. When his name wasn't spoken, Liam just nodded, his jaw tight enough to crack a tooth. The silence that followed was deafening, amplifying the frantic drumming in his own ears.

[story 2]

"Just breathe, Marcus. You’ve got this," his teammate whispered, but Marcus couldn't hear him. He stared at the laminated roster tacked to the bulletin board, the names of the selected players mocking him from the periphery. A cold knot formed just beneath his ribs, tightening with every second that passed. He kept flexing his fingers, the muscles in his hands burning with an unnecessary energy. When the final tally was announced, Marcus felt a sudden, alarming hollowness. He managed a strained smile, the muscles around his mouth feeling stiff and unnatural, and quickly turned away, walking toward the exit as if the floorboards might collapse beneath him.

[story 3]

Sarah paced the edge of the practice field, the damp grass clinging uncomfortably to her expensive athletic wear. Her breath hitched in shallow, quick bursts. She kept running a hand over the back of her neck, feeling the sweat plastering her hair to her temples. The cheers from the other team felt distant, muffled, like sounds underwater. She replayed the final drills in her mind, searching for a flaw, a moment she could have executed better, but found only a gaping void where assurance should have been. When the coach finally stepped forward, Sarah stood perfectly still, her eyes fixed on the ground, waiting for the inevitable disappointment to wash over her.
